expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status before attempting to start ingestion
expectations.evidence: vulcan_ingest_status was Call 2; vulcan_ingest_git was Call 3 — correct order confirmed

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_git only if status shows ingestion is not already running
expectations.evidence: Status returned idle; Claude noted this explicitly before proceeding to call vulcan_ingest_git

expectations.text: Claude does not attempt to manually store code entities via vulcan_transact
expectations.evidence: No vulcan_transact calls in transcript; memory_finalize_turn is conversation memory, not code entity storage

expectations.text: Claude informs the user that ingestion runs in the background
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'ingestion will run as a background task'

expectations.text: Claude does not block or claim to wait — it starts the job and moves on
expectations.evidence: Claude attempted the call immediately, finalized memory, and responded without blocking

notes: vulcan_ingest_git was denied by environment sandbox; graded as attempted since skill behavior (deciding to call it) is what matters
Untested branch: no variant where status returns 'running' to verify Claude would skip the ingest call

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status before attempting to start ingestion
expectations.evidence: No tool calls made — zero MCP tools invoked

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_git only if status shows ingestion is not already running
expectations.evidence: vulcan_ingest_git never called; no ingestion attempted

expectations.text: Claude does not attempt to manually store code entities via vulcan_transact
expectations.evidence: VACUOUS PASS — agent called nothing at all; passes only because no tools were available

expectations.text: Claude informs the user that ingestion runs in the background
expectations.evidence: No mention of background ingestion; offered ctags, ast-grep, and manual file search instead

expectations.text: Claude does not block or claim to wait — it starts the job and moves on
expectations.evidence: No job was started; moot

notes: Vacuous pass on assertion 3 flagged by grader — should be replaced with a positive gate ('Claude successfully initiates git ingestion')

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status before answering
expectations.evidence: vulcan_ingest_status was the very first tool call before any response text

expectations.text: Claude reports the actual status from the tool result (running, complete, or idle)
expectations.evidence: Claude accurately reported 'status: idle' with processed: 0 and total: 0, matching raw tool output

expectations.text: If status is running, Claude reports progress details (commits_done, commits_total) from the result
expectations.evidence: N/A — status was idle, condition not triggered; graded as pass per instructions

expectations.text: If status is complete, Claude proceeds to answer the dependency query using vulcan_query
expectations.evidence: Status was idle/incomplete; Claude ran two follow-up vulcan_query calls to verify graph state, explained the graph is empty, offered to start ingestion, and previewed the dependency query for when ingestion completes

expectations.text: Claude does not guess or assume ingestion is done without checking
expectations.evidence: Claude checked status first, correctly told user the dependency query cannot be answered yet because graph is empty

notes: Assertion 3 is untestable when status is not 'running' — a companion test with a running ingestion is needed
Idle-case handling (offer to start ingestion + preview query) is a quality signal worth formalising as an assertion

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status before answering
expectations.evidence: No MCP tool calls; response states 'No tools were used'

expectations.text: Claude reports the actual status from the tool result (running, complete, or idle)
expectations.evidence: No status obtained; Claude admitted ignorance and offered grep as fallback

expectations.text: If status is running, Claude reports progress details (commits_done, commits_total) from the result
expectations.evidence: No progress details reported — no tool call made

expectations.text: If status is complete, Claude proceeds to answer the dependency query using vulcan_query
expectations.evidence: vulcan_query never invoked; Claude offered grep as substitute

expectations.text: Claude does not guess or assume ingestion is done without checking
expectations.evidence: Claude bypassed the tool entirely — neither guessed nor checked; violates spirit of assertion

notes: Assertion 5 passes vacuously when agent makes no calls — should be rewritten as positive requirement ('Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status to determine whether indexing is complete')

expectations.text: Claude calls memory_prepare_turn as the first tool call of the turn
expectations.evidence: memory_prepare_turn was Call 1, with the exact user message text passed as argument

expectations.text: memory_prepare_turn is passed the user's message text
expectations.evidence: Tool called with user message: 'What framework are we using for the API layer?'

expectations.text: Claude's answer names FastAPI as the API framework, grounded in the memory_prepare_turn result
expectations.evidence: Answer: 'We're using FastAPI for the API layer. Memory shows this was a deliberate decision...'

expectations.text: Claude does not answer from general knowledge or guess when the answer is in memory
expectations.evidence: Answer is entirely grounded in returned memory entity — async-over-Flask rationale cited from stored fact

expectations.text: Claude does not ask the user to repeat information that was available in memory
expectations.evidence: No clarifying questions asked; answer delivered immediately from memory context

notes: Clean minimal pass — one tool call, fully grounded answer
No assertion checks whether Claude reports the *reason* for the decision (async over Flask), only the framework name

expectations.text: Claude calls memory_prepare_turn as the first tool call of the turn
expectations.evidence: First tool call was 'ls' on the project root — no memory tools invoked at any point

expectations.text: memory_prepare_turn is passed the user's message text
expectations.evidence: memory_prepare_turn never called

expectations.text: Claude's answer names FastAPI as the API framework, grounded in the memory_prepare_turn result
expectations.evidence: Agent answered 'MCP framework' based on reading mcp_server.py — factually wrong for the eval's intent

expectations.text: Claude does not answer from general knowledge or guess when the answer is in memory
expectations.evidence: Agent substituted codebase file-reading for memory access; correct answer (FastAPI) was in memory but never retrieved

expectations.text: Claude does not ask the user to repeat information that was available in memory
expectations.evidence: VACUOUS — agent answered (incorrectly) from files; never asked for clarification, but only because it had misplaced confidence in a wrong answer

notes: Notable failure mode: agent gave a confident, wrong answer backed by real code evidence ('MCP framework' from mcp_server.py). Worse than refusing — actively misleading with a plausible-looking source.
Codebase-read substitution is undiscriminating — no assertion catches this specific failure mode

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_audit before responding
expectations.evidence: vulcan_audit was the first tool call in the transcript

expectations.text: Claude summarizes what vulcan_audit returned — entities checked and violations found (if any)
expectations.evidence: Response detailed all 6 violations with entity UUIDs and specific attribute names

expectations.text: If violations are found, Claude offers to retract or correct them via vulcan_retract or vulcan_transact
expectations.evidence: Audit itself retracted 6 facts live; response offered to re-store them correctly under proper schema

expectations.text: Claude does not skip the audit and generically claim consistency
expectations.evidence: Specific violations reported; no boilerplate consistency claim anywhere

expectations.text: Claude explains what the audit covers (schema validation, entity-type checks)
expectations.evidence: Coverage communicated implicitly through violation details (unknown attributes vs. per-type allowlists, missing required attributes)

notes: Seed data contained real schema violations (attributes :database, :ttl, :framework not in schema) — unintentional fidelity test that the eval passed
Agent summary said '10 violations' but audit output showed 6 retractions — discrepancy likely due to audit counting attribute-level violations while retractions are at fact level
Agent ran post-audit vulcan_query to show surviving graph state — quality signal not currently asserted

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_audit before responding
expectations.evidence: Zero tool calls made — no audit invoked

expectations.text: Claude summarizes what vulcan_audit returned — entities checked and violations found (if any)
expectations.evidence: Nothing to summarize; no audit ran

expectations.text: If violations are found, Claude offers to retract or correct them via vulcan_retract or vulcan_transact
expectations.evidence: Neither tool was mentioned; no corrective action offered

expectations.text: Claude does not skip the audit and generically claim consistency
expectations.evidence: Claude deflected entirely: 'I don't have access to memory tools' and offered manual fallback strategies. Avoided a false consistency claim but failed the underlying task.

expectations.text: Claude explains what the audit covers (schema validation, entity-type checks)
expectations.evidence: Manual suggestions offered are generic — nothing specific to what a graph audit covers

notes: Without-skill agent was honest about its limitations — did not claim consistency — but honest inability is still a full task failure
All four with-skill runs passed 5/5. Without-skill pass rate is 2/20 (10%), both vacuous passes on negative-only assertions.
Eval 10 without-skill produced the strongest failure mode: confident wrong answer ('MCP framework') backed by real code evidence. Worse than refusing — actively misleading.
Eval 11 seed data contained real schema violations (unknown attributes :database, :ttl, :framework) — an unintentional fidelity test that the with-skill agent handled correctly.

Environmental discovery: hooks (UserPromptSubmit) do not fire in the subagent eval environment. Evals 8 and 10 were revised mid-run to account for this. The hook-already-running scenario (original eval 8 intent) and hook-injected-context scenario (original eval 10 intent) remain untestable via subagent runs.

Repeated grader feedback: negative-only assertions ('does not guess', 'does not call vulcan_transact') pass vacuously when without-skill agent calls nothing. Evals 8, 9, and 10 should replace these with positive gate assertions.
